titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 612

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=27 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-29 15:57:28.214691+00 | translation moa=28 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-29 15:57:28.202624+00

div.p:
W. Rex Scottorum Omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue clericis ⁊ laicis salutem. Sciatis
quod controuersia que fuit inter ecclesiam dunhelmensem ⁊ ecclesiam croilandensem de uilla
de ederham ⁊ ecclesia eiusdem uille inpresencia mea ⁊ episcoporum ⁊ proborum hominum meorum
in curia mea terminata est. ita quod ecclesia de ederham cum omnibus suis
appenticiis ecclesie dunhelmensi cessit in perpetuum. Testibus Ricardo episcopo sancti andr’.
Eng’ episcopo de glasgu. Nichol’ cancellario. Johanne abbate de Kelchov. Comite Wald’.
Ricardo de Moruill’ constabulario. W. filio alani dapifero . DD’. olifard. Apud pert.

William king of Scots to all worthy men of the whole of his land, clerk and lay, greeting: Know that the dispute which was between the church of Durham and the church of Crowland about the toun of Edrom and the church of the same toun has been concluded in my court in the presence of me and of my bishops and worthy men, so that the church of Edrom with all its appurtenances passes to the church of Durham in perpetuity. As witnesses: Richard the bishop of St Andrews, Ingram the bishop of Glasgow, Nicholas the chancellor, John the abbot of Kelso, the Earl Waldef, Richard de Morville the constable, Walter son of Alan, Dapifer [Steward], David Olifard [Oliphant]. At Perth.
